beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and prepare for the day
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning start; Henry needs his ritual clarity before the day unfolds. Low energy yesterday (30/100) means he'll move deliberately and not rush.

beats.activity: Swim at Beacon Hill Pool to start the day right
beats.reasoning: Henry can't start the day without a swim—it's his anchor ritual. Sunday morning is perfect timing, pool will be quieter than weekdays, and the calm sunny weather makes it especially appealing.
Short walk back to Beacon Hill home; budget 20 min for the return and a moment to decompress post-swim.

beats.activity: Breakfast and coffee at home, check in on garden seedlings
beats.reasoning: Frugal and easily distracted—Henry makes breakfast at home rather than spending money out. Urban gardening is a key hobby, so tending to seedlings on the back porch is natural Sunday morning behavior.

beats.activity: Head to community garden to water plants and tend tomato beds
beats.reasoning: Henry's urban gardening passion draws him out on a sunny Sunday. The warm, breezy weather (72F high) is perfect for garden work. He's been tending tomatoes all week and they're coming in—this is a grounding activity after yesterday's high-energy pride event.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby café for brunch and people-watching
beats.reasoning: Henry likes his oat milk lattes and quiet moments observing the city. After the intensity of yesterday (Pride), a low-key brunch near home fits his mood and energy recovery needs. Frugal but treats himself to a café meal.

beats.activity: Return home, read, and rest on the porch
beats.reasoning: Energy is still low (yesterday 30/100). Sunday afternoon is prime time for Henry to recharge with a book or quiet reflection. The sunny weather invites porch time. Values recovery after social events.

beats.activity: Light lunch: sandwich and fruit at home
beats.reasoning: Frugal, stays home for lunch. Keeps it simple on a low-energy Sunday. No need to go out again.

beats.activity: Text close friend and walk to nearby park for casual hangout
beats.reasoning: Henry values time with close friends over big crowds. Lake Union has heavy recreational traffic today, so a park walk is a good alternative. Volunteer Park is accessible and offers quiet green space to catch up with a friend one-on-one.
Return to Beacon Hill. Budget 45 min for the walk back from Capitol Hill; gives Henry time to decompress and enjoy the sunshine.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, watch a show, unwind at home
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening at home. Henry cooks a simple dinner (frugal), relaxes with a show or podcast. Sunday wind-down before the work week. Low energy still calls for in-home comfort.

beats.activity: Evening routine: tidy up, prep for the week, read in bed
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down beat before sleep. Henry organizes his week, lays out clothes for Monday, and settles into bed with a book. Natural transition to sleep.

diary:
Sunday felt like a gift—one of those rare days where everything just lined up right. Started with an early swim at Beacon Hill Pool; the water was perfect and I had the lane mostly to myself. There's something about those quiet morning laps that clears my head before the week even starts.

Spent the middle of the day pottering around Beacon Hill—watered the tomato beds at the community garden (they're finally looking robust), grabbed brunch at Café Solstice and just watched the world go by for a bit. The weather was so nice I couldn't resist meeting up with Marcus at Volunteer Park for a few hours. We didn't do much, just sat on a bench, talked about nothing important, threw breadcrumbs at pigeons. Felt restorative somehow.

Came home as the light started going golden, made a proper dinner, watched something mindless on the couch. There's a contentment sitting in my chest right now—the kind that comes from a day well-spent doing mostly nothing at all.
thought_process: I think I needed this kind of day more than I realized. Work as a school counselor can be emotionally heavy, and Sundays are my reset button. The weather being perfect probably helped—I found myself naturally moving toward outdoor spaces, which I don't always prioritize. The swim set a good tone early, and instead of forcing productivity, I let myself follow what felt right: the garden, the café, the park with Marcus. I didn't overthink the schedule or try to optimize anything. By evening I was genuinely tired in that good way, ready to prep mentally for the week ahead. Sometimes the best self-care is just permission to be a bit aimless.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
